content.explanation:
**Conjunctions — Joining Words (जोड़ने वाले शब्द):**

Conjunctions दो words, phrases, या clauses को जोड़ते हैं।

**COORDINATING CONJUNCTIONS (FANBOYS):**
• **F**or, **A**nd, **N**or, **B**ut, **O**r, **Y**et, **S**o

**AND** = और (adding info): I study English **and** Hindi.
**BUT** = लेकिन (contrast): I tried hard **but** failed.
**OR** = या (choice): Tea **or** coffee?
**SO** = इसलिए (result): I was tired, **so** I slept.
**YET** = फिर भी: He is rich, **yet** he is unhappy.

**SUBORDINATING CONJUNCTIONS:**
**BECAUSE** = क्योंकि: I stayed home **because** it rained.
**ALTHOUGH** = हालाँकि: **Although** it was late, she continued working.
**HOWEVER** = हालाँकि/लेकिन (formal): It is expensive; **however**, it is worth it.
**THEREFORE** = इसलिए (formal): He was late; **therefore**, he missed the bus.
**UNLESS** = जब तक नहीं: **Unless** you try, you will not succeed.
**WHILE** = जबकि / जब: I study **while** she cooks.
rules: Coordinating conjunctions (FANBOYS) join equal elements (words, phrases, clauses)
Subordinating conjunctions join a main clause with a dependent clause
Because = reason; Although = contrast; Unless = condition (negative)
However, Therefore, Moreover = used after semicolons (;) in formal writing
Although/Though/Even though = similar meaning (though is informal)
content.memoryTrick:
**FANBOYS** = For, And, Nor, But, Or, Yet, So
एक आसान trick: Because = Reason, Although = Surprise, Unless = Condition, Therefore = Result
Conjunctions = Bridges between ideas!

examples.hindi: मैं थका था लेकिन काम करता रहा।
examples.english: I was tired but I kept working.

examples.hindi: वह देर से आई क्योंकि traffic था।
examples.english: She arrived late because there was traffic.

examples.hindi: हालाँकि मुझे खाना पसंद नहीं था, मैंने खाया।
examples.english: Although I did not like the food, I ate it.

examples.hindi: उसने खूब पढ़ा, इसलिए pass हो गया।
examples.english: He studied a lot; therefore, he passed.

examples.hindi: जब तक मेहनत नहीं करोगे, success नहीं मिलेगी।
examples.english: Unless you work hard, you will not succeed.

examples.hindi: यह expensive है; हालाँकि, quality बेहतरीन है।
examples.english: It is expensive; however, the quality is excellent.

mistakes.correct: However, it was difficult. / But it was difficult.
mistakes.why: But and However mean the same — use only one

mistakes.wrong: Although she tried, but she failed.
Because of he was late, he missed the bus.
mistakes.correct: Because he was late, he missed the bus.
mistakes.why: 'Because' is a conjunction — follow with subject + verb, not 'of'

mistakes.wrong: She is smart, so but she got the job.
mistakes.correct: She is smart, so she got the job.

speakingTips: Complex sentences बनाओ: 'Although I was tired, I finished the work because the deadline was important.'
Business: 'The project was delayed; however, the quality was not compromised.'
Story: 'I was about to leave when it started raining, so I stayed inside.'

vocabulary.example: She is talented; moreover, she is hardworking.
It was raining; nevertheless, we went out.
He did not practice; consequently, he failed.
I cooked; meanwhile, he set the table.
She is quiet, whereas he is loud.
I will help, provided you are honest.

practice.hindi: मैं सेब और केला खाता हूँ।
practice.english: I eat apples and bananas.
alternatives: i have apples and bananas.

practice.hindi: वह होशियार है लेकिन आलसी है।
practice.english: She is intelligent but lazy.
alternatives: she is smart but idle.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम चाय लोगे या कॉफी?
practice.english: Will you have tea or coffee?
alternatives: do you want tea or coffee?

practice.hindi: बारिश हो रही थी इसलिए मैं घर रहा।
practice.english: It was raining so I stayed home.
alternatives: it was raining so i didn't go out.

practice.hindi: मैं खुश हूँ क्योंकि मैंने परीक्षा पास की।
practice.english: I am happy because I passed the exam.
alternatives: i am happy since i cleared the test.

practice.hindi: मुझे भूख लगी है इसलिए मैं कुछ खाऊँगा।
practice.english: I am hungry so I will eat something.
alternatives: i feel hungry so i will have something.

practice.hindi: वह थका हुआ था फिर भी उसने काम किया।
practice.english: Although he was tired, he worked.
alternatives: though he was tired, he kept working.

practice.hindi: हालाँकि वह अमीर है, वह खुश नहीं है।
practice.english: Although he is rich, he is not happy.
alternatives: even though he is wealthy, he is unhappy.

practice.hindi: अगर तुम मेहनत करोगे, तो सफल होगे।
practice.english: If you work hard, you will succeed.
alternatives: if you put in effort, you will achieve success.

practice.hindi: जब तक तुम नहीं मेहनत करोगे, सफल नहीं होगे।
practice.english: Unless you work hard, you will not succeed.
alternatives: you will not succeed unless you work hard.

practice.hindi: जब वह आई, मैं सो रहा था।
practice.english: When she came, I was sleeping.
alternatives: i was asleep when she arrived.

practice.hindi: वह खाना बना रही थी जब मैंने फोन किया।
practice.english: She was cooking while I called.
alternatives: i called while she was cooking.

practice.hindi: जब तक वह नहीं आएगा, हम इंतज़ार करेंगे।
practice.english: We will wait until he comes.
alternatives: we shall wait till he arrives.

practice.hindi: जब से वह गई है, घर सुनसान लग रहा है।
practice.english: Since she left, the house feels empty.
alternatives: the house feels lonely since she left.

practice.hindi: खाना खाने के बाद वह सो गया।
practice.english: After eating food, he slept.
alternatives: he went to sleep after having food.

practice.hindi: सोने से पहले दाँत ज़रूर साफ़ करो।
practice.english: Before sleeping, always brush your teeth.
alternatives: always brush your teeth before you sleep.

practice.hindi: वह थका हुआ था फिर भी उसने हार नहीं मानी।
practice.english: He was tired yet he did not give up.
alternatives: he was tired but he still did not quit.

practice.hindi: न मैं जाऊँगा, न वह जाएगी।
practice.english: Neither I will go nor will she.
alternatives: i will not go and she will not either.

practice.hindi: वह अच्छी तरह पढ़ता है इसलिए वह हमेशा पास होता है।
practice.english: He studies well, for he always passes.
alternatives: he always passes because he studies well.

practice.hindi: वह होशियार है और मेहनती भी है।
practice.english: She is intelligent and hardworking too.
alternatives: she is smart and also dedicated.

practice.hindi: मैं जानता था कि वह आएगा।
practice.english: I knew that he would come.
alternatives: i was sure that he would arrive.

practice.hindi: वह इतनी तेज़ चिल्लाई कि सब घबरा गए।
practice.english: She screamed so loudly that everyone got scared.
alternatives: she shouted so much that people panicked.

practice.hindi: मैं घर जाऊँगा जब तुम बुलाओगे।
practice.english: I will go home when you call.
alternatives: i will leave when you call me.

practice.hindi: तुम जहाँ भी जाओ, मैं साथ चलूँगा।
practice.english: Wherever you go, I will come with you.
alternatives: i will follow you wherever you go.

practice.hindi: जितनी जल्दी तुम शुरू करोगे, उतना बेहतर होगा।
practice.english: The sooner you start, the better it will be.
alternatives: the earlier you begin, the better.
